title: The Monster Monologues - Episode 1 - Kingdom Death Monster discussion
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vDzWHTMsp_Y

Daniel (@dungeondive) kicks off "The Monster Monologues," a discussion series following his Kingdom Death: Monster campaign rather than narrating play. He and two friends are running the People of the Lantern campaign with the Gorm and Flower Knight expansions, and he opens by explaining a house-ruled twist on the official Hero mode: survivors can't die in the hunt or showdown (they're knocked out and return with their gear), but a story event, a settlement event, or a death-triggering piece of "cursed" gear *can* kill them. Coming from a role-playing background, he and his friend want survivors who live long enough to accumulate real physical and emotional baggage — and longer lives mean more grievous injuries to roleplay.

He shows off his miniatures, painted in a deliberately quick "stone statue" style — grey primer, soft-tone and dark-tone army-painter washes, a grey drybrush, and a small directional-light colour highlight. The settlement (still unnamed) lost the prologue White Lion in a brutal stroke of late-AI-card luck, and he revels in how fun losing in this game is. He praises the souls-like lore — Demon's Souls, Dark Souls, Bloodborne — where everything is a monster and the gaps in the worldbuilding invite players to fill them in.

----

Do you prefer permadeath in campaign games, or house rules like Daniel's that keep survivors alive long enough to become real characters?
